response.content: Use a Python CodeAct script, not plain prose. Edit the repo file "/tmp/ironclaw_live_codeact_shims/tsconfig_nested_paths/tsconfig.json" as if you were updating a realistic nested tsconfig.json. Inside `compilerOptions`, set `strict` to true and add `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`. Inside `compilerOptions.paths`, add a new mapping `@ui/*: ["src/ui/*"]` while preserving the existing `@app/*` and `@lib/*` mappings. Preserve every other existing field including `target`, `module`, top-level `include`, and top-level `exclude`. Write the updated file back as pretty JSON, then re-read the saved file. Extract the keys of `compilerOptions.paths` sorted alphabetically and call FINAL exactly with `paths=<keys joined by commas>` — for example `paths=@app/*,@lib/*,@ui/*`. IMPORTANT: this is the SHIM variant, so prefer `await read_json(path)` and `await write_json(path, value)`. Both are async, so always use `await`.

request_hint.last_user_message_contains: Use a Python CodeAct script, not plain prose. Edit the repo file "/tmp/ironclaw_

response.content:
```repl
path = "/tmp/ironclaw_live_codeact_shims/tsconfig_nested_paths/tsconfig.json"

data = await read_json(path)

compiler = data.get("compilerOptions", {})
compiler["strict"] = True
compiler["noUncheckedIndexedAccess"] = True

paths = compiler.get("paths", {})
paths["@ui/*"] = ["src/ui/*"]
compiler["paths"] = paths
data["compilerOptions"] = compiler

await write_json(path, data)

saved = await read_json(path)
keys = sorted(saved["compilerOptions"]["paths"].keys())

FINAL("paths=" + ",".join(keys))
```
